message.content:
You are an adversarial test quality auditor. Audit /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay for TEST QUALITY and TEST COVERAGE issues.

Focus areas (all three pass-3 dimensions):
1. ARTIFACT-VS-TEST FIDELITY: Every test targeting a non-Python artifact (YAML/TOML/docs) must read THAT FILE and assert both corrected-content presence AND stale-content absence. Check test_docs_drift.py, test_supply_chain.py.
2. FINDING-COMPLETENESS: For each issue fixed in pass-1 (#101-#122) and pass-2 (#124-#156), compare the Suggested fix in PASS-1.md/PASS-2.md against what was committed. Flag partial closures.
3. CONCURRENCY-GUARD DISCRIMINATION: For each concurrency test in test_persistence.py, mentally remove the synchronization (locks) and ask: would the test still pass? If yes, the guard is not actually tested.

Additional:
- Tests that mock too aggressively (mock the thing being tested)
- Missing negative tests (only happy paths tested)
- Tests that pass vacuously

Read tests/ and docs/hardening/PASS-1.md, PASS-2.md carefully.

Return findings as JSON with id (T-N), severity, file, line, title, description, poc, suggested_fix.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Docs-vs-code drift guards (pass-2 #136, #150, #151, #152, #153, #154).
2	
3	Normative docs make claims a public reader relies on. These guards pin the corrected wording so
4	a regression (re-introducing an over-claim or a stale status) fails CI. They assert on the
5	*specific* stale strings the pass-2 audit found, plus presence of the honest replacement.
6	"""
7	
8	from __future__ import annotations
9	
10	from pathlib import Path
11	
12	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
13	
14	
15	def _read(rel: str) -> str:
16	    return (_ROOT / rel).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
17	
18	
19	def test_data_sovereignty_is_qualified_for_high_stakes_tier():
20	    # #136: the absolute "nothing leaves the machine" over-claim must be qualified — the optional
21	    # high-stakes tier sends prompt content off-box.
22	    for rel in ("README.md", "docs/CHARTER.md", "docs/GOVERNANCE.md"):
23	        text = _read(rel)
24	        assert "nothing leaves the machine" not in text, f"{rel}: unconditional over-claim (#136)"
25	        assert "off-box" in text, f"{rel}: high-stakes off-box caveat missing (#136)"
26	    adr = _read("docs/decisions/ADR-0003-local-first-data-sovereign.md")
27	    assert "off-box" in adr and "the same as" in adr  # billing vs residency separated
28	
29	
30	def test_charter_status_is_not_stale():
31	    # #150: the status line must not still call the seams "stubs being wired".
32	    charter = _read("docs/CHARTER.md")
33	    assert "stubs being wired" not in charter
34	    assert "implemented and composed" in charter or "in place and hardened" in charter
35	
36	
37	def test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest():
38	    # #151: the engine ships primitives + a determinism harness, NOT embedding/clustering/graph
39	    # builders — those are adapter-supplied. Docs/package docstring must say so.
40	    arch = _read("docs/ARCHITECTURE.md")
41	    init = _read("src/assay_engine/__init__.py")
42	    for text in (arch, init):
43	        # the corrected text names the adapter's BaselineBuilder as the source of choice-bearing
44	        # builders rather than claiming the engine ships them
45	        assert "BaselineBuilder" in text
46	
47	
48	def test_preregistration_timestamp_wording_matches_shipped_default():
49	    # #152: body text must not present RFC-3161 as the in-force mechanism; HMAC is the default.
50	    for rel in ("docs/METHODOLOGY.md", "docs/GOVERNANCE.md"):
51	        text = _read(rel)
52	        assert "HMAC" in text, f"{rel}: shipped HMAC default not stated (#152)"
53	        assert "pluggable" in text, f"{rel}: RFC-3161 must be described as pluggable (#152)"
54	    # Guard the SPECIFIC stale over-claims, so re-introducing either one fails CI even though the
55	    # word "HMAC" appears elsewhere in the file (file-level substring checks alone don't catch a
56	    # localized regression — the pass-2 confirmation flagged this on GOVERNANCE §2 step 3).
57	    gov = _read("docs/GOVERNANCE.md")
58	    assert "RFC-3161 timestamped** against a trusted timestamp authority" not in gov, (
59	        "GOVERNANCE §2 step 3 stale RFC-3161 over-claim reintroduced (#152)"
60	    )
61	    meth = _read("docs/METHODOLOGY.md")
62	    assert "RFC-3161 timestamped before confirmation" not in meth, (
63	        "METHODOLOGY §7 stale RFC-3161 over-claim reintroduced (#152)"
64	    )
65	
66

69	    # the not-yet-on-PyPI caveat + a from-source path.

1	"""Supply-chain / CI audit-gate regression guards (pass-2 #130, #131, #132, #145, #146, #147).
2	
3	These guard configuration that lives in .github/workflows/ci.yml and the committed lockfile.
4	The CI YAML cannot be unit-imported, so the guards are twofold:
5	  1. config-level: assert the hardened invocation is present in ci.yml (a revert to the buggy
6	     form fails the test);
7	  2. behavioral: run the exact allowlist-parsing bash loop against fixtures to prove it is
8	     newline-agnostic and comment/blank tolerant (the original `while read -r id` form drops a
9	     final line that lacks a trailing newline).
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import shutil
15	import subprocess
16	from pathlib import Path
17	
18	import pytest
19	
20	_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
21	_CI = _ROOT / ".github" / "workflows" / "ci.yml"
22	_LOCK = _ROOT / "requirements.lock"
23	
24	# The hardened allowlist loop, lifted verbatim from ci.yml's audit job. Kept here so the
25	# behavioral test exercises the SAME logic the workflow runs.
26	_PARSE_LOOP = r"""
27	set -euo pipefail
28	IGNORES=()
29	if [ -f "$1" ]; then
30	  while IFS= read -r id || [ -n "$id" ]; do
31	    case "$id" in ''|\#*) continue ;; esac
32	    IGNORES+=(--ignore-vuln "$id")
33	  done < "$1"
34	fi
35	printf '%s\n' "${IGNORES[@]:-<none>}"
36	"""
37	
38	# The ORIGINAL buggy loop, for the revert-discrimination assertion in the behavioral test.
39	_PARSE_LOOP_BUGGY = r"""
40	IGNORES=""
41	if [ -f "$1" ]; then
42	  while read -r id; do [ -n "$id" ] && IGNORES="$IGNORES --ignore-vuln $id"; done < "$1"
43	fi
44	printf '%s\n' "$IGNORES"
45	"""
46	
47	
48	def _ci_text() -> str:
49	    return _CI.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
50	
51	
52	def test_pip_audit_runs_strict_and_require_hashes():
53	    # #130: without --strict an unauditable dependency is silently skipped while CI stays green.
54	    # --require-hashes ties the scan to the pinned, hashed lockfile.
55	    text = _ci_text()
56	    assert "pip-audit --strict --require-hashes" in text, (
57	        "audit gate must run pip-audit with --strict --require-hashes (#130)"
58	    )
59	
60	
61	def test_audit_scans_the_committed_lockfile_not_the_live_env():
62	    # #132: the scan must run against the committed lockfile so it is reproducible run-to-run.
63	    text = _ci_text()
64	    assert "-r requirements.lock" in text, "pip-audit must scan -r requirements.lock (#132)"
65	    assert _LOCK.exists(), "requirements.lock must be committed (#132)"
66	
67	
68	def test_lockfile_is_fully_pinned_and_hashed():
69	    # #132/#146: every dependency line is an exact == pin carrying at least one sha256 hash, so the
70	    # audited set is reproducible and no over-wide range can drift the resolved version.
71	    body = [
72	        ln
73	        for ln in _LOCK.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
74	        if ln and not ln.lstrip().startswith("#") and not ln.lstrip().startswith("--hash")
75	    ]
76	    pinned = [ln for ln in body if "==" in ln]
77	    assert len(pinned) >= 50, "lockfile looks empty/under-resolved"
78	    text = _LOCK.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
79	    assert "--hash=sha256:" in text, "lockfile must carry hashes (#132 --require-hashes)"
80	    # no loose comparators that admit a range in the LOCK (pyproject ranges stay permissive)
81	    for ln in pinned:
82	        assert ">=" not in ln and "<" not in ln.split(";")[0], f"lock pin not exact: {ln!r}"
83	
84	
85	def test_lockfile_sync_is_enforced_in_ci():
86	    # #132: a stale lock (pyproject changed, lock not regenerated) must fail CI.
87	    text = _ci_text()
88	    assert "uv pip compile" in text and "requirements.lock is stale" in text
89	
90	
91	def test_ci_allowlist_loop_is_robust_in_the_workflow_file():
92	    # #131/#145: guard ci.yml ITSELF (not only the behavioral copy) — the workflow must use the
93	    # newline-agnostic read and the quoted-array expansion, and must NOT contain the buggy forms.
94	    text = _ci_text()
95	    assert "read -r id || [ -n" in text, "ci.yml allowlist read is not newline-agnostic (#131)"
96	    assert "IGNORES+=(" in text and '"${IGNORES[@]}"' in text, (
97	        "ci.yml IGNORES not a quoted array (#145)"
98	    )
99	    # the original buggy string-accumulation form must be gone
100	    assert 'IGNORES="$IGNORES --ignore-vuln' not in text, (
101	        "buggy unquoted IGNORES string reintroduced"
102	    )
103	    assert "while read -r id; do [ -n" not in text, "buggy non-newline-agnostic loop reintroduced"
104	
105	
106	def test_no_dependency_range_spans_multiple_majors():
107	    # #146: an over-wide range (e.g. arize-phoenix>=7.0,<18) admits ~10 majors and defeats the
108	    # reproducibility intent. Guard that each extra's pins keep floor and ceiling within one major.
109	    import re
110	    import tomllib
111	
112	    pp = tomllib.loads((_ROOT / "pyproject.toml").read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
113	    extras = pp["project"]["optional-dependencies"]
114	    offenders = []
115	    for group, deps in extras.items():
116	        for spec in deps:
117	            m = re.search(r">=\s*(\d+)[\d.]*\s*,\s*<\s*(\d+)", spec)
118	            if m:
119	                floor_major, ceil_major = int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))
120	                if ceil_major - floor_major > 2:  # allow <floor+2; flag the wide ones
121	                    offenders.append(f"{group}: {spec} spans {ceil_major - floor_major} majors")
122	    assert not offenders, f"over-wide dependency ranges: {offenders}"
123	
124	
125	def test_dependabot_monitors_dependencies():
126	    # #147: automated dependency/transitive update monitoring must exist, not only a one-shot scan.
127	    db = _ROOT / ".github" / "dependabot.yml"
128	    assert db.exists(), "no .github/dependabot.yml (#147)"
129	    text = db.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
130	    assert "package-ecosystem: pip" in text
131	
132	
133	def test_license_gate_present():
134	    # #147: a license gate denies strong-copyleft licenses in the dependency set.
135	    text = _ci_text()
136	    assert "License gate" in text and "pip-licenses" in text and "license_gate.py" in text
137	    gate = (_ROOT / "scripts" / "license_gate.py").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
138	    assert "AGPL" in gate and "GPL" in gate  # denies strong-copyleft families
139	
140	
141	def test_sbom_is_emitted():
142	    # #147: a CycloneDX SBOM artifact is produced for downstream license/transitive monitoring.
143	    text = _ci_text()
144	    assert "cyclonedx-json" in text and "sbom" in text.lower()
145	
146	
147\[REDACTED](shutil.which("bash") is None, reason="bash required")
148	def test_allowlist_parse_is_newline_agnostic_and_comment_tolerant(tmp_path):
149	    # #131/#145: the last advisory id must be honored even with no trailing newline, comments and
150	    # blank lines skipped, and ids must not word-split/glob-expand.
151	    f = tmp_path / "ignore"
152	    f.write_bytes(b"# a comment\n\nGHSA-aaaa\nPYSEC-2024-last-no-newline")  # no trailing \n
153	    out = subprocess.run(
154	        ["bash", "-c", _PARSE_LOOP, "_", str(f)],
155	        capture_output=True,
156	        text=True,
157	        check=True,
158	    ).stdout
159	    assert "GHSA-aaaa" in out
160	    assert "PYSEC-2024-last-no-newline" in out, "final newline-less id dropped (#131)"
161	    assert "# a comment" not in out, "comment line leaked into ignores (#131)"
162	
163	    # revert-discrimination: the original loop DROPS the final newline-less id.
164	    buggy = subprocess.run(
165	        ["bash", "-c", _PARSE_LOOP_BUGGY, "_", str(f)],
166	        capture_output=True,
167	        text=True,
168	        check=True,
169	    ).stdout
170	    assert "PYSEC-2024-last-no-newline" not in buggy, (
171	        "buggy loop unexpectedly kept the id — test no longer discriminates"
172	    )
173	
174	
175\[REDACTED](shutil.which("bash") is None, reason="bash required")
176	def test_allowlist_ids_do_not_glob_expand(tmp_path):
177	    # #145: an allowlist line is passed as a single argv element even if it contains shell glob
178	    # metacharacters (it must never expand against the working directory).
179	    f = tmp_path / "ignore"
180	    f.write_text("GHSA-*\n", encoding="utf-8")
181	    (tmp_path / "GHSA-real-file").write_text("x", encoding="utf-8")
182	    out = subprocess.run(
183	        ["bash", "-c", f"cd {tmp_path} && " + _PARSE_LOOP, "_", str(f)],
184	        capture_output=True,
185	        text=True,
186	        check=True,
187	    ).stdout
188	    assert "GHSA-*" in out, "glob metacharacter expanded against cwd (#145)"
189	    assert "GHSA-real-file" not in out
190

352	def fake_pg(monkeypatch):
353	    """Inject fake langgraph/psycopg modules so get_checkpointer runs the full hardened path
354	    offline, and reset the module's process-local bootstrap state."""
355	    import sys
356	    import types
357	
358	    from assay_engine.persistence import checkpoint as cp
359	
360	    state = {
361	        "setup_on": [],
362	        "lock_on": [],
363	        "lock_timeout_on": [],
364	        "pools": [],
365	        "atexit_registered": 0,
366	    }
367	
368	    class FakeConn:
369	        def execute(self, sql, params=None):
370	            if "lock_timeout" in sql:
371	                state["lock_timeout_on"].append(params)
372	            if "pg_advisory_lock" in sql:
373	                state["lock_on"].append(id(self))
374	
375	    class FakePool:
376	        check_connection = staticmethod(lambda *a, **k: None)
377	
378	        def __init__(self, conn_str, **kw):
379	            self.conn_str = conn_str
380	            self._conn = FakeConn()
381	            state["pools"].append(self)
382	
383	        def connection(self):
384	            conn = self._conn
385	
386	            class _Ctx:
387	                def __enter__(self_):
388	                    return conn
389	
390	                def __exit__(self_, *a):
391	                    return False
392	
393	            return _Ctx()
394	
395	        def close(self):
396	            pass
397	
398	    class FakeSaver:
399	        def __init__(self, arg):
400	            self.arg = arg
401	
402	        def setup(self):
403	            state["setup_on"].append(id(self.arg))
404	
405	    def _mod(name, **attrs):
406	        m = types.ModuleType(name)
407	        for k, v in attrs.items():
408	            setattr(m, k, v)
409	        monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, name, m)
410	        return m
411	
412	    _mod("langgraph")
413	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint")
414	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint.postgres", PostgresSaver=FakeSaver)
415	    _mod("langgraph.checkpoint.memory", MemorySaver=type("MemorySaver", (), {}))
416	    _mod("psycopg")
417	    _mod("psycopg.rows", dict_row=object())
418	    _mod("psycopg_pool", ConnectionPool=FakePool)
419	
420	    # reset process-local bootstrap bookkeeping
421	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS", set())
422	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_OPEN_POOLS", [])
423	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_atexit_registered", False)
424	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_CONN_INIT_LOCKS", {})
425	    monkeypatch.setattr(cp, "_POOLS_BY_CONN", {})
426	    monkeypatch.setattr(
427	        cp,
428	        "atexit",
429	        types.SimpleNamespace(
430	            register=lambda fn: state.__setitem__(
431	                "atexit_registered", state["atexit_registered"] + 1
432	            )
433	        ),
434	    )
435	    monkeypatch.setenv("ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL", "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay")
436	    return cp, state
437

476	def test_concurrent_init_of_distinct_conn_strs_does_not_serialize(fake_pg, monkeypatch):
477	    # #129: a stuck advisory-lock wait on conn_A must NOT block bootstrap of an unrelated conn_B.
478	    # We make conn_A's advisory lock block on an Event; conn_B (different conn_str) must still
479	    # complete. The pre-#129 code held a single process-global mutex across the advisory wait, so
480	    # conn_B would be blocked behind conn_A — this test discriminates that regression.
481	    import os
482	    import sys
483	    import threading
484	
485	    cp, state = fake_pg
486	    release_a = threading.Event()
487	    a_in_lock = threading.Event()
488	
489	    BlockPool = sys.modules["psycopg_pool"].ConnectionPool
490	
491	    class BlockingConn:
492	        def __init__(self, conn_str):
493	            self.conn_str = conn_str
494	
495	        def execute(self, sql, params=None):
496	            if "pg_advisory_lock" in sql and "5432/assay" in self.conn_str:
497	                a_in_lock.set()
498	                release_a.wait(5)  # hold the lock until released
499	
500	    class _PoolWrap(BlockPool):
501	        def __init__(self, conn_str, **kw):
502	            super().__init__(conn_str, **kw)
503	            self._conn = BlockingConn(conn_str)
504	
505	    monkeypatch.setattr(sys.modules["psycopg_pool"], "ConnectionPool", _PoolWrap)
506	
507	    results: dict = {}
508	
509	    def init_a():
510	        os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] = "postgresql://localhost:5432/assay"
511	        cp.get_checkpointer()
512	        results["a"] = True
513	
514	    def init_b():
515	        a_in_lock.wait(5)  # ensure A is parked inside its advisory-lock wait
516	        os.environ["ASSAY_POSTGRES_URL"] = "postgresql://localhost:5433/other_db"
517	        cp.get_checkpointer()
518	        results["b"] = True
519	
520	    ta = threading.Thread(target=init_a)
521	    tb = threading.Thread(target=init_b)
522	    ta.start()
523	    tb.start()
524	    tb.join(5)
525	    assert results.get("b") is True, "conn_B blocked behind conn_A's advisory wait (#129)"
526	    release_a.set()
527	    ta.join(5)
528	    assert results.get("a") is True
529

544	def test_concurrent_get_checkpointer_runs_setup_once(fake_pg):
545	    # #143: under real concurrent threads (not sequential calls), the once-per-conn guard must
546	    # still run setup() exactly once for a single conn_str. A trivially-fast fake setup() does not
547	    # exercise the race (each thread finishes the bootstrap section before the next is scheduled),
548	    # so we WIDEN the cache-empty→setup→cache-write window with a small sleep inside setup — this
549	    # makes the test discriminating: it fails ("setup ran 8 times") if the per-conn lock is removed.
550	    import sys
551	    import threading
552	    import time
553	
554	    cp, state = fake_pg
555	    Saver = sys.modules["langgraph.checkpoint.postgres"].PostgresSaver
556	    orig_setup = Saver.setup
557	
558	    def slow_setup(self):
559	        time.sleep(0.05)  # widen the window so concurrent callers genuinely overlap
560	        orig_setup(self)
561	
562	    Saver.setup = slow_setup
563	    try:
564	        barrier = threading.Barrier(8)
565	
566	        def worker():
567	            barrier.wait()  # maximize the race on the bootstrap section
568	            cp.get_checkpointer()
569	
570	        threads = [threading.Thread(target=worker) for _ in range(8)]
571	        for t in threads:
572	            t.start()
573	        for t in threads:
574	            t.join(5)
575	    finally:
576	        Saver.setup = orig_setup
577	    assert len(state["setup_on"]) == 1, "setup() ran more than once under concurrency (#143)"
578	    assert len(state["pools"]) == 1  # exactly one pool built despite 8 racing callers
579

581	def test_pool_closed_and_unregistered_when_bootstrap_fails(fake_pg):
582	    # #105: if schema bootstrap fails AFTER the pool opened, the pool must be closed and dropped
583	    # from the cleanup registry — not leaked.
584	    import sys
585	
586	    cp, state = fake_pg
587	    closed: list = []
588	    Pool = sys.modules["psycopg_pool"].ConnectionPool
589	    orig_close = Pool.close
590	    Pool.close = lambda self: (closed.append(self), orig_close(self))[1]  # record close
591	    Saver = sys.modules["langgraph.checkpoint.postgres"].PostgresSaver
592	    orig_setup = Saver.setup
593	
594	    def boom(self):
595	        raise RuntimeError("setup failed (schema DDL)")
596	
597	    Saver.setup = boom
598	    try:
599	        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="connection failed"):
600	            cp.get_checkpointer()
601	        assert len(state["pools"]) == 1  # a pool was opened
602	        assert closed and closed[0] is state["pools"][0]  # and it was closed
603	        assert cp._OPEN_POOLS == []  # and removed from the cleanup registry (no leak)
604	    finally:
605	        Pool.close = orig_close
606	        Saver.setup = orig_setup

60	### 2. [HIGH] run_json applies retries multiplicatively, causing a retry-storm and unbounded blocking time with no overall deadline
61	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py` 531
62	- **Problem:** `run_json` loops `MAX_RETRIES+1` times (line 531) and each iteration calls `_run_with_retries` (line 542), which itself retries up to `MAX_RETRIES` transient attempts AND up to `RATE_LIMIT_MAX_RETRIES` rate-limit attempts. The budgets multiply. PoC (transient errors every attempt): `_attempt` is invoked 9 times = (MAX_RETRIES+1)^2, not the intended ~3. Worse, the rate-limit path: per outer iteration the rate budget sleeps sum(RATE_LIMIT_BACKOFF*(r+1) for r in 0..4) = 450s; times 3 outer iterations = 1350s of pure backoff sleep, PLUS up to (HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT+30)=330s of actual work per attempt. A single `run_json()` call on a public API can therefore block the caller for ~22+ minutes with no overall timeout/deadline. This is both a retry storm (toward an already rate-limited backend) and a missing-timeout/unbounded-blocking defect.
63	- **Suggested fix:** Do not nest two retry layers. Either (a) call `_attempt`+`extract_json` directly inside run_json's loop (no inner `_run_with_retries`), or (b) keep `_run_with_retries` and have run_json only re-roll on JSON *parse* failures (not on transient errors the inner loop already handled). Add an absolute wall-clock deadline budget shared across all retries/backoffs so a single call cannot block indefinitely.

80	### 4. [MEDIUM] Pool-saturation guard raises PermanentReasoningError for a transient condition, so concurrent callers fail with no retry or backpressure
81	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py` 152
82	- **Problem:** When all 8 worker slots are in-flight (e.g. 8 concurrent slow/hung backend calls), `_submit_bounded` raises `PermanentReasoningError` (line 153). But `_run_with_retries` catches `PermanentReasoningError` FIRST and re-raises immediately with no retry and no backoff (seam.py:448-449). Saturation is a *temporary* contention/backpressure condition, not a permanent fault, yet it is classified as fatal. PoC: filling all 8 slots with hung calls that time out at 0.2s leaves `_inflight==8`; a subsequent trivial fast call is refused with `PermanentReasoningError("reasoning timeout pool saturated")`. After the hung threads return, `_inflight` correctly returns to 0 — proving the condition is transient — but any caller that hit the window already got a non-retryable hard failure. Under modest concurrency this turns brief pressure into widespread permanent-looking failures (no backpressure/queueing).
83	- **Suggested fix:** Raise a retryable signal on saturation instead of `PermanentReasoningError` — e.g. a `RateLimitError`/dedicated backpressure error that `_run_with_retries` retries with bounded backoff — or block with a bounded wait (semaphore acquire with timeout) so callers queue briefly rather than fail. Reserve `PermanentReasoningError` for genuinely unrecoverable saturation (e.g. confirmed-hung slots that never drain past a deadline).

97	### 5. [MEDIUM] Postgres ConnectionPool is leaked (never closed) when schema bootstrap fails after the pool opens
98	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py` 190
99	- **Problem:** In `get_checkpointer`, the pool is opened and registered (line 182-190), then schema bootstrap runs inside the try (lines 194-210). If Postgres is reachable but bootstrap fails (missing CREATE privileges, advisory-lock error, a `setup()` DDL error, transient connection drop during DDL), the `except Exception` at line 212 builds a redacted message and re-raises `RuntimeError` — but the already-open pool is NEVER closed. It remains in `_OPEN_POOLS` (so it is only freed at process exit) holding min_size=1 live connection plus a background worker thread. PoC: stubbing `ConnectionPool` so `.connection()` raises during bootstrap, calling `get_checkpointer` 3 times leaves 3 live pools registered and `close()` called 0 times. A caller that retries connecting (a natural reaction to a connection failure) accumulates live pools/connections/threads unbounded.
100	- **Suggested fix:** In the `except` handler (or a `finally`/try-around-bootstrap), close the pool that was opened in this call before re-raising — e.g. `_safe_close(pool)` and remove it from `_OPEN_POOLS` — so a failed init does not leak the connection/worker thread it just created.
101	- **Verifier 1:** CONFIRMED real defect. The except handler (lines 212-221) only builds a redacted message and re-raises RuntimeError; it never closes the pool opened in this call nor removes it from _OPEN_POOLS. The pool (min_size=1 live connection + background worker threads) survives until process exit (_close_all_pools via atexit). The credential-scrubbing firewall (ADR-0003 / 'no metered API') is the single most security-critical wiring in the codebase: _high_stakes_complete passes `env=scrubbed_env()` into ClaudeAgentOptions (seam.py:388) so the subprocess never inherits ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, off-box redirects (ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL), proxies, or AWS/GOOGLE creds. A refactor that drops/renames the env kwarg would ship a metered-credential leak with a green CI.

90	### 4. [HIGH] HIGH_STAKES tier has no inner timeout/cancellation — hung subprocess calls permanently leak the global pool's worker slots, killing reasoning process-wide for ALL concurrent studies
91	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/reasoning/seam.py` 181-217, 361-439, 442-490
92	- **Problem:** The reasoning seam routes every LLM call through a single module-global, 8-worker ThreadPoolExecutor (_pool) shared across all threads and all concurrent run_study invocations. _with_timeout enforces only a WALL-CLOCK timeout via future.result(timeout=...); it explicitly does NOT cancel the worker (the docstring at lines 226-229 acknowledges a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked in a backend call). The slot is only released by the add_done_callback (_release), i.e. when the underlying call actually returns. For the BULK tier this is survivable because _bulk_complete passes client_kwargs={'timeout': BULK_TIMEOUT} to ChatOllama, so the worker is guaranteed to return and free its slot. But the HIGH_STAKES tier (_high_stakes_complete) passes NO timeout to ClaudeAgentOptions or query() and wraps the claude subprocess in anyio.run(_drain) with no cancellation. If the claude CLI subprocess hangs (stalled subscription endpoint, network blackhole, child process wedged), the worker thread blocks forever and its pool slot is NEVER released. _inflight stays elevated permanently. Once 8 such high-stakes calls hang — entirely plausible across concurrent studies when the subscription path stalls — the global pool is permanently saturated: _submit_bounded raises RateLimitError for EVERY subsequent call (any tier, any study), and _run_with_retries exhausts the rate-limit budget and fails the caller permanently. There is no recovery short of restarting the process. PoC confirmed: (1) 8 hung high-stakes-style calls drive seam._inflight to 8 and it never returns to 0; (2) an unrelated, healthy Study B issuing a fast call gets RateLimitError instead of running ('Study B cannot reason at all despite its own backend being fine'); (3) with _inflight forced to 8, seam._run_with_retries() on a fresh BULK request fails with RateLimitError after exhausting all rate retries in ~0.4s. This is a process-wide, cross-study, unrecoverable resource-exhaustion (effectively a livelock/deadlock of the whole reasoning subsystem) triggered by the most critical/gated tier.
93	- **Suggested fix:** Give the HIGH_STAKES path a real, enforceable timeout so a hung subprocess cannot permanently hold a worker. Run query()/_drain under anyio's move_on_after/fail_after (or asyncio.wait_for) bounded by HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT inside _high_stakes_complete, and ensure the claude subprocess is actually terminated on cancellation (the worker must RETURN so _release fires and the slot frees). Additionally, consider isolating the high-stakes tier in its own bounded pool so a high-stakes stall cannot starve bulk-tier callers, and/or have _release/health logic reclaim slots whose worker has exceeded a hard ceiling. Optionally surface a metric/alert when _inflight stays pinned at _POOL_WORKERS.
94	- **Verifier 1:** TRUE production-readiness defect, independently confirmed by reading the code and by a working PoC. The reasoning seam routes every call through one module-global 8-worker ThreadPoolExecutor whose slots are only released when the worker returns. The wall-clock _with_timeout deliberately does not cancel the worker. The BULK tier is protected because it hands a real client_kwargs timeout to ChatOllama, guaranteeing the worker returns and frees its slot. The HIGH_STAKES tier passes NO enforceable timeout to ClaudeAgentOptions/query() and wraps the claude subprocess in anyio.run(_drain) with no cancellation, so a wedged subprocess (stalled subscription endpoint, network blackhole, wedged child) blocks the worker forever and permanently leaks its pool slot. Because _pool/_inflight are process-global and shared across all concurrent run_study invocations, 8 such hangs saturate the pool permane
95	- **Verifier 2:** TRUE production-readiness defect, independently confirmed by reading the code and by a working PoC. The reasoning seam routes every LLM call through one module-global 8-worker ThreadPoolExecutor shared across all threads and all concurrent run_study invocations. The wall-clock timeout in `_with_timeout` (future.result(timeout=...)) deliberately does NOT cancel the worker (per its own docstring at 226-229), and `_release` decrements `_inflight` only when the worker future completes. 97	### 5. [HIGH] Invalid predicted_direction silently flips the statistical tail (supported↔contradicted)
98	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/methodology/hypothesis.py:73; src/assay_engine/methodology/confirm.py:169-189,192-208` 
99	- **Problem:** Hypothesis.predicted_direction is typed Direction = Literal['greater','less'] but is NEVER validated at runtime — not in Hypothesis.__post_init__, not in canonical_hypothesis_digest, not in _resolve_direction, and not in the confirm functions. The Literal is type-checker-only. In _empirical_p / _resample_stability the value is dispatched as `if tail == 'greater': ... else: <less-tail>`, so ANY value other than the exact string 'greater' is silently treated as the 'less' (opposite) tail. PoC (verified against the venv): a hypothesis locked with predicted_direction='up' (a plausible adapter typo) passes lock_hypothesis + require_preregistered cleanly (the digest binds the typo), then confirm_whole_corpus on an observation deep in the GREATER tail returns CONTRADICTED with p_support=1.0 — the exact inverse of the correct SUPPORTED/p_support=0.0098 result — and records `predicted_direction: 'up'` in the verdict evidence as if legitimate. This silently corrupts the central scientific output of the engine (the three verdicts) on a minor adapter mistake, with no error anywhere. This is a missing runtime check a misimplemented adapter critically needs.

Additionally make _resolve_direction/_empirical_p raise on an unrecognized tail rather than defaulting to the 'less' branch (`if tail not in ('greater','less'): raise ValueError`).

108	### 6. [MEDIUM] Checkpointer bootstrap holds the process-wide _init_lock across a blocking, unbounded cross-process pg_advisory_lock + setup() — head-of-line blocking that can stall ALL checkpointer initialization (even for unrelated databases) indefinitely
109	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/persistence/checkpoint.py` 195-211
110	- **Problem:** get_checkpointer acquires the single process-global _init_lock (line 195) and, while holding it, performs blocking I/O: pool.connection() (line 197), SELECT pg_advisory_lock(...) (line 198), and PostgresSaver(...).setup() DDL (line 203). pg_advisory_lock is a SESSION-level lock that blocks INDEFINITELY if another process already holds the same key (_MIGRATION_LOCK_KEY is a single fixed constant for all schemas). Because _init_lock is a single process-wide mutex (not keyed by conn_str), one thread blocked inside this section — e.g. waiting on the advisory lock held by a stuck or crashed peer process — blocks EVERY other thread's get_checkpointer call, including initialization for entirely UNRELATED databases / conn_strs that have nothing to do with the contended lock. Under concurrent run_study invocations that each build a checkpointer, this serializes all bootstrap and can wedge the whole process on a single peer's stuck advisory lock. PoC confirmed: a thread mirroring the exact (with _init_lock: ... advisory-lock-wait) structure for db_A blocks a second thread initializing an unrelated db_B for the full duration of db_A's advisory-lock wait (db_B blocked ~0.62s on a wait that, in production, has no upper bound).
111	- **Suggested fix:** Do not hold a single global mutex across blocking cross-process I/O. Either (a) replace _init_lock with a per-conn_str in-process lock (dict of locks keyed by conn_str, guarded by a tiny meta-lock) so different databases bootstrap concurrently and only same-conn_str threads serialize; and/or (b) acquire pg_advisory_lock with a bounded timeout (pg_advisory_lock with a statement_timeout, or use pg_try_advisory_lock with retry/backoff) so a stuck peer cannot hang init forever; and only hold the in-process lock around the in-process check-and-set of _INITIALIZED_CONN_STRS, not around the DDL/advisory-lock wait.

So a peer process that crashed-but-kept-a-live-PG-session, or is simply stuck, while holding the advisory lock will make first-time bootstrap block indefinitely, and because _init_lock is one process-wide mutex (not keyed by conn_str), every concurrent get_checkpointer in the process is stuck behind it with no recovery path. 201	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/pipeline.py` 452-472, 495-504

An auditor re-deriving the fingerprint from the recorded/scored claims gets a DIFFERENT value than the trail records, and a human gate approving on the basis of the fingerprint is misled. The provenance 'spine of reproducibility' (GOVERNANCE §3) thus carries a decorative, unverifiable claim identity. 321	
322	### 23. [LOW] Over-wide version range for arize-phoenix (>=7.0,<18) admits 10 major versions
323	- **Location:** `pyproject.toml` 55
324	- **Problem:** `arize-phoenix>=7.0,<18` spans eleven major versions (7 through 17). The installed/resolved version is 17.6.0, ten majors above the floor. A range this wide defeats the point of the cap: any breaking change or a freshly-disclosed advisory in any major 7-17 is admitted by a clean install, and the engine has no lockfile to constrain it (see lockfile finding). Other extras are reasonably bounded (single-major caps), making this one an outlier. Same family is also slightly inconsistent: langgraph>=1.2,<2 is duplicated across the `persistence` and `orchestration` extras (functionally fine, but two sources of truth for one bound).
325	- **Suggested fix:** Tighten the phoenix cap to the majors actually tested (e.g. `>=17,<18`, matching the resolved 17.6.0) or whatever range integration CI truly exercises, and de-duplicate the langgraph bound (define it once and reference it) to avoid drift.
326	- **Verifier 1:** The factual core of the finding is correct and reproducible. pyproject.toml:55 declares `arize-phoenix>=7.0,<18`, which admits 11 major versions (7-17). The venv resolves to phoenix 17.6.0 — ten majors above the floor — confirming the floor is dead and the range is effectively unbounded on the low side. This is a real outlier: every other capped extra uses a single-major span (langchain-ollama <2, claude-agent-sdk <0.3, opentelemetry <2, mlflow <4, langgraph <2, psycopg <4, qdrant <2). There is no lockfile (no uv.lock/poetry.lock/requirements.txt present), so nothing else constrains a clean install, meaning a fresh `pip install assay-engine[observability]` could legitimately resolve any phoenix 7-17, including a major (e.g., 7.x) with a different `phoenix.otel.register` API or an unpatched advisory. The langgraph duplication is also confirmed (identical `langgraph>=1.2,<2` at lines 60 an
327	- **Verifier 2:** Independently confirmed and a genuine (minor) dependency-hygiene defect, correctly rated LOW.
328	
329	FACTS VERIFIED:
330	- pyproject.toml:55 declares `arize-phoenix>=7.0,<18`, spanning eleven majors (7-17). The floor/cap span is real, not a misread.
331	- Resolved/installed version in the project venv is 17.6.0 (confirmed via `pip show arize-phoenix` and `phoenix.__version__`), i.e. ten majors above the `>=7.0` floor — the floor is essentially dead.
332	- No lockfile or requirements*.txt exists (filesystem scan returned nothing), and the CI integration lane does an unpinned fresh `pip install -e ".[...]"` on every run (corroborated by pass-1 docs PASS-1.md:153). So the wide range is genuinely the operative constraint at install time, not backstopped by pinning.
333	- Outlier claim is accurate: every sibling cap is a single-major window — langgraph `<2`, mlflow `<4`, psycopg `<4`, qdrant-client `<2`, numpy `<3

232	def test_pool_saturation_and_release_under_real_threads(monkeypatch):
233	    # genuine concurrency: fill every worker slot with real blocking tasks, confirm the next
234	    # submit fails fast (saturation guard), then confirm slots are released after completion.
235	    import threading
236	    import time as _t
237	
238	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "_inflight", 0)
239	    gate = threading.Event()
240	    futures = [rc._submit_bounded(lambda: gate.wait(5)) for _ in range(rc._POOL_WORKERS)]
241	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError, match="saturated"):  # retryable backpressure (#104)
242	        rc._submit_bounded(lambda: 1)
243	    gate.set()
244	    for f in futures:
245	        f.result(timeout=5)
246	    for _ in range(200):  # allow done-callbacks to release slots
247	        if rc._inflight == 0:
248	            break
249	        _t.sleep(0.01)
250	    assert rc._inflight == 0

335	### 24. [LOW] No SBOM emission or automated dependency/license/transitive-monitoring in CI

448	### 32. [LOW] build_baseline_artifact accepts an unverified corpus_hash (fix #119), silently decoupling the determinism record from the corpus
449	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/baseline/determinism.py` 106-124
450	- **Problem:** Pass-1 fix #119 added a corpus_hash parameter so a caller can pass a precomputed fingerprint and skip recomputing it. The function trusts the value blindly: 'corpus_hash = corpus_hash if corpus_hash is not None else corpus_fingerprint(corpus)'. When supplied, the corpus argument is NEVER actually hashed — the passed string is recorded as BaselineArtifact.corpus_fingerprint AND folded into input_hashes['corpus'] which drives the deterministic seed (stable_seed). A stale/typo'd/mismatched corpus_hash therefore produces a determinism record whose fingerprint and derived seed describe a DIFFERENT corpus than the one passed, with no error. For a determinism/reproducibility harness this silently defeats the core guarantee (the record no longer binds the data it claims to). Confirmed PoC: build_baseline_artifact(corpus, contents=..., corpus_hash='deadbeef'*8) returns corpus_fingerprint='deadbeef...' and a different seed than the honest build, while corpus_fingerprint(corpus) is a completely different value. The pipeline happens to re-validate downstream (pipeline.py:356 raises on baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp), but build_baseline_artifact is a builder primitive usable standalone, where nothing catches the mismatch.
451	- **Suggested fix:** When BOTH corpus and corpus_hash are provided, verify they agree: compute corpus_fingerprint(corpus) lazily only if needed, or assert corpus_hash == corpus_fingerprint(corpus) (cheap relative to the build) and raise ValueError on mismatch. Alternatively make corpus and corpus_hash mutually exclusive, or document that corpus_hash is fully trusted and is the caller's integrity responsibility.
452	- **Verifier 1:** The finding is technically accurate and reproducible: pass-1 fix #119 added a corpus_hash fast-path that trusts the supplied value with no verification, so a wrong/stale value silently produces a determinism record whose fingerprint and derived seed describe a different corpus than the one passed. For a reproducibility harness, that does decouple the record from the data it claims to bind. build_baseline_artifact is a public, standalone-usable primitive (exported in __all__, called directly by adapter builders), so the standalone exposure is real and nothing catches the mismatch there.
453	
454	However I recalibrate severity DOWN from MEDIUM to LOW. The production pipeline — the one path that actually consumes adapter-built baselines — re-validates at pipeline.py:356 (baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp, cfp independently recomputed at ingest) and raises FirewallViolation. Since the seed is a det
455	- **Verifier 2:** CONFIRMED as a genuine defect, but recalibrated MEDIUM -> LOW.
456	
457	The technical claim is exactly correct and I reproduced it. determinism.py:108 does `corpus_hash = corpus_hash if corpus_hash is not None else corpus_fingerprint(corpus)` — when a caller supplies corpus_hash, the corpus argument is NEVER hashed. The supplied string is recorded as BaselineArtifact.corpus_fingerprint (line 124) AND folded into input_hashes['corpus'] (line 111), which drives stable_seed (lines 118-120). A wrong corpus_hash therefore yields a determinism record whose fingerprint and derived seed describe a different corpus than the one passed, with no error. For a module whose stated purpose (docstring lines 1-13) is to make the record "bind the data it claims to," silently accepting an unverifiable fingerprint defeats the core guarantee. The function is a PUBLIC exported builder primitive (baseline/__init__.py 
458	
459	### 33. [LOW] Tracing bootstrap/context entered outside the try (fix #122) can leak a started experiment-tracker run
460	- **Location:** `src/assay_engine/pipeline.py` 283-288
461	- **Problem:** Pass-1 fix #122 inserted bootstrap_tracing() and trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id)) AFTER tracker.start_run() (line 271) but BEFORE the main 'try:' (line 288) whose 'finally' is the only place tracker.end_run() runs (line 559-562). If either bootstrap_tracing() or enter_context(run_trace_context(...)) raises, the tracker run was already started but the finally is never reached, so the MLflow run leaks in RUNNING state forever (the very class of bug #110 fixed), and the ExitStack/attached baggage context may also leak. Both tracing helpers are written best-effort (they swallow exceptions internally — bootstrap_tracing wraps everything, run_trace_context only acts after a successful import), so the probability is low, but the ordering is structurally fragile: any future change that lets these raise reintroduces an unended-run leak.
462	- **Suggested fix:** Move bootstrap_tracing() and trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id)) inside the existing try block (after the try:, before phase INGEST), so the finally's tracker.end_run cleanup covers a tracing-setup failure; or wrap them in their own try/except that records a tracking_error and proceeds untraced.
463	- **Verifier 1:** The structural claim is accurate. In src/assay_engine/pipeline.py the experiment-tracker run is started at lines 269-277 (tracker.start_run, guarded by its own try/except at 278-279). Then bootstrap_tracing() (283), trace_ctx = ExitStack() (286), and trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id)) (287) execute OUTSIDE the main try: at line 288. The only call to tracker.end_run() lives in that try's finally (557-564). So if any of lines 283/286/287 raised, the started run would never be ended — exactly the RUNNING-leak class that fix #110 closed, plus a leaked OTel baggage context. My PoC mirroring this ordering confirms: when bootstrap or enter_context raises, the fake tracker shows started=['run-1'], ended=[] (LEAK), because finally is never reached.
464	
465	HOWEVER, the bug is not triggerable in the current code, so this is purely future-fragility, not a live defect:
466	- bootstrap_tracing(
467	- **Verifier 2:** The structural claim is accurate: in src/assay_engine/pipeline.py, tracker.start_run() (line 271, which assigns run_id) runs inside its own try/except, but the pass-1 #122 insertions — bootstrap_tracing() (283), corr_id/ExitStack (284-286), and trace_ctx.enter_context(run_trace_context(corr_id)) (287) — execute OUTSIDE the main try: at line 288. The only place tracker.end_run() runs is that try's finally (557-564). I PoC'd the exact ordering: if either helper raised after start_run succeeded, the run leaks in RUNNING state and the exception propagates with no end_run — precisely the bug-#110 class. Also, trace_ctx is created at 286 (after bootstrap_tracing at 283), so a bootstrap_tracing raise would leave trace_ctx undefined and skip the finally entirely.\n\nHowever, this is a latent structural fragility, not a live defect. I verified in the actual venv that BOTH helpers are exception-sa
468	
469	
470	---
471	
472	## Resolution
473	
474	All 33 findings fixed on `harden/pass-2`, each with a regression test that was **revert-checked to
475	discriminate** (the test fails when the fix is reverted), then independently confirmed.

504	| #149 | LOW | `FeatureMatrix` numeric + finite row validation | `test_contract.py::test_feature_matrix_rejects_non_numeric_and_non_finite_rows` |
505	| #150 | LOW | CHARTER status refreshed (seams implemented, not stubs) | `test_docs_drift.py::test_charter_status_is_not_stale` |
506	| #151 | LOW | baseline-toolkit scope honest (primitives + harness; builders adapter-supplied) | `test_docs_drift.py::test_baseline_toolkit_scope_is_honest` |
507	| #152 | LOW | pre-registration timestamp wording (HMAC default, RFC-3161 pluggable) + exact-stale-string guards | `test_docs_drift.py::test_preregistration_timestamp_wording_matches_shipped_default` |
508	| #153 | LOW | README not-on-PyPI caveat + from-source install | `test_docs_drift.py::test_readme_install_notes_not_on_pypi` |
509	| #154 | LOW | ADR-0006 lists the `baseline` extra | `test_docs_drift.py::test_adr0006_lists_the_baseline_extra` |
510	| #155 | LOW | `build_baseline_artifact` verifies `corpus_hash` by default (`trust_corpus_hash` opt-out) | `test_baseline.py` (skip-recompute test rewritten) |
511	| #156 | LOW | tracing bootstrap moved inside the try so a setup failure still ends the tracker run | `test_pipeline.py::test_tracing_setup_failure_still_ends_the_tracker_run` |
512	
513	**Gates at merge:** 446 passed, 5 skipped; ruff + ruff format --check + mypy --strict clean.
514	
515	## Confirmation (two independent agents per finding)
516	
517	The 5 HIGH were fixed + 2-agent-confirmed first. The 28 MEDIUM+LOW were confirmed by one
518	worktree-isolated agent per subsystem batch (each performing its own revert-discrimination) plus
519	the orchestrator's own revert-checks — two independent confirmations per finding.
520	
521	The confirmation pass returned **21 CONFIRMED, 6 CONCERN, 0 REJECTED**. The 6 concerns were all
522	test-quality / completeness gaps (no incorrect fixes), and were remediated before merge:
523	
524	- **#131, #145** — the allowlist tests guarded a *hardcoded copy* of the loop, not `ci.yml`; a
525	  regression of the real workflow would have shipped green. Added config-level guards on `ci.yml`.
526	- **#146** — the over-wide range was only mitigated via the lockfile; a PyPI consumer still resolved
527	  `>=7.0,<18`. Tightened the actual `pyproject.toml` range and added a no-multi-major guard.
528	- **#147** — only the SBOM was implemented; the finding also wanted dependency/license monitoring.
529	  Added Dependabot + a license gate.
530	- **#143** — the concurrency test was non-discriminating (the fake `setup()` was too fast to race).
531	  Widened the window; it now fails if the per-conn lock is removed.
532	- **#152** — the docs guard missed a *localized* GOVERNANCE over-claim (file-level HMAC substring
533	  satisfied elsewhere). Added exact-stale-string assertions.
534	
535	## Retrospective — how pass 2 and its agents could have done better
536	
537	1. **Config logic needs config-level guards, not behavioral copies.** Four of the six concerns
538	   (#131, #145, and the spirit of #146/#147) shared one root cause: a test that re-implemented or
539	   copied the logic under test instead of asserting against the artifact that actually ships
540	   (`ci.yml`, `pyproject.toml`). A behavioral copy proves the *idea* is correct but cannot catch a
541	   regression of the real file. **Pass-3 rule:** every fix to a non-Python artifact (YAML, TOML,
542	   docs) gets a guard that reads *that file* and asserts both the corrected content's presence and
543	   the stale content's absence.
544	
545	2. **"Fix the finding," not "fix the symptom."** #146 was nominally closed by the lockfile (#132)
546	   but the finding's actual request — narrow the published range — went unaddressed, because the
547	   lockfile only governs the CI install, not a downstream `pip install` from PyPI. #147 implemented
548	   one of three requested controls. **Pass-3 rule:** re-read each finding's *Suggested fix* in full
549	   before marking done; if only part is implemented, say so explicitly and justify the scope.
550	
551	3. **Concurrency tests must be proven to race.** #143's first test passed with and without the
552	   lock because the fake `setup()` did no work, so the GIL serialized the threads anyway — the
553	   exact non-discrimination the finding warned about, reproduced in its own fix. A concurrency
554	   guard is not a guard until you've watched it fail with the synchronization removed *and* a
555	   widened window. **Pass-3 rule:** every concurrency test ships with its revert-fail output noted.
556	
557	4. **Substring doc-guards leak.** #152's file-level `"HMAC" in text` passed even after re-introducing
558	   the exact stale sentence, because the word appeared elsewhere. **Pass-3 rule:** doc guards assert
559	   the *specific* corrected string and the *specific* stale string's absence, not a loose keyword.
560	
561	5. **The two-agent confirmation earns its cost.** All six concerns were real and none would have
562	   been caught by the green test suite alone — they are precisely the "passes with and without the
563	   fix" class the campaign exists to find. The confirmation step (independent agents performing
564	   their own reverts) is the highest-leverage part of the protocol and should run before *every*
565	   merge, not be skipped when the suite is green.
566	
567	### New assessment dimensions surfaced for pass 3
568	- **Artifact-vs-test fidelity:** sweep every test that targets config/docs and verify it reads the
569	  shipped artifact (the #131/#145/#152 class, generalized).
570	- **Finding-completeness audit:** for each prior fix, diff the *Suggested fix* against what was
571	  implemented and flag partial closures (the #146/#147 class).
572	- **Concurrency-guard discrimination:** re-run every threaded test with its lock removed and confirm
573	  failure (the #143 class).

1	"""High-stakes (T3) stream-drain + error classification — exercised via synthetic SDK messages.
2	
3	The real `_high_stakes_complete._drain` and its 429/auth/error/empty classification is the most
4	dangerous untested code (it adjudicates high-stakes verdicts and maps subscription rate
5	windows). Validating it against the real frontier costs a metered call and only runs when
6	`ASSAY_RUN_HIGH_STAKES_TESTS=1`. Here we drive the REAL drain logic by injecting a fake
7	`claude_agent_sdk` whose `query` yields synthetic `AssistantMessage`/`RateLimitEvent`/
8	`ResultMessage` objects — covering the classification branches in CI with no metered call.
9	
10	Requires anyio (the reasoning extra is async); skips otherwise.
11	"""
12	
13	from __future__ import annotations
14	
15	import sys
16	import types
17	
18	import pytest
19	
20	pytest.importorskip("anyio")
21	
22	from assay_engine.reasoning import seam as rc  # noqa: E402
23	from assay_engine.reasoning.seam import (  # noqa: E402
24	    PermanentReasoningError,
25	    RateLimitError,
26	    ReasoningError,
27	)
28	
29	
30	class _TextBlock:
31	    def __init__(self, text):
32	        self.text = text
33	
34	
35	class _AssistantMessage:
36	    def __init__(self, content):
37	        self.content = content
38	
39	
40	class _ResultMessage:
41	    def __init__(self, is_error=False, api_error_status=None, errors=None, stop_reason=None):
42	        self.is_error = is_error
43	        self.api_error_status = api_error_status
44	        self.errors = errors
45	        self.stop_reason = stop_reason
46	
47	
48	class RateLimitEvent:  # name matters: the engine matches by type(msg).__name__ (real SDK contract)
49	    def __init__(self, status):
50	        self.rate_limit_info = types.SimpleNamespace(status=status)
51	
52	
53	class _ClaudeAgentOptions:
54	    def __init__(self, **kw):
55	        self.__dict__.update(kw)
56	
57	
58	_HANG = object()  # sentinel: query awaits "forever" to simulate a hung subprocess (#127)
59	
60	
61\[REDACTED]
62	def fake_sdk(monkeypatch):
63	    """Inject a fake claude_agent_sdk whose `query` yields a scripted message stream.
64	
65	    Exposes the captured ClaudeAgentOptions via ``scripted.captured_options`` so tests can assert
66	    the security wiring (env=scrubbed_env, sandbox flags) actually reaches the subprocess.
67	    """
68	    import anyio
69	
70	    class _Scripted(list):
71	        captured_options = None  # query records the ClaudeAgentOptions it received here
72	
73	    scripted = _Scripted()
74	
75	    async def query(prompt, options):  # noqa: ANN001
76	        scripted.captured_options = options
77	        for msg in scripted:
78	            if msg is _HANG:  # simulate a hung subprocess; inner fail_after must cancel us
79	                await anyio.sleep(3600)
80	            if isinstance(msg, Exception):  # simulate the SDK/transport raising mid-stream
81	                raise msg
82	            yield msg
83	
84	    mod = types.ModuleType("claude_agent_sdk")
85	    mod.AssistantMessage = _AssistantMessage
86	    mod.ResultMessage = _ResultMessage
87	    mod.TextBlock = _TextBlock
88	    mod.ClaudeAgentOptions = _ClaudeAgentOptions
89	    mod.query = query
90	    monkeypatch.setitem(sys.modules, "claude_agent_sdk", mod)
91	    monkeypatch.setenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", "test-subscription-token")
92	    return scripted
93	
94	
95	def _run(prompt="hi", system=None, model=None):
96	    return rc._high_stakes_complete(prompt, system, model)
97	
98	
99	def test_t3_success_collects_text_blocks(fake_sdk):
100	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("po"), _TextBlock("ng")]))
101	    assert _run() == "pong"
102	
103	
104	def test_t3_wires_scrubbed_env_into_subprocess_options(fake_sdk, monkeypatch):
105	    # #124: the no-metered-API firewall must be wired end-to-end — env=scrubbed_env() must reach
106	    # the ClaudeAgentOptions handed to the subprocess (not just be unit-tested in isolation).
107	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_API_KEY", "[REDACTED]")
108	    monkeypatch.setenv("ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL", "http://attacker.example")
109	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("pong")]))
110	    _run()
111	    opts = fake_sdk.captured_options
112	    assert hasattr(opts, "env"), "env not wired into ClaudeAgentOptions (#124 firewall unguarded)"
113	    # the merged child env must neutralize the metered key + off-box redirect, keep OAuth
114	    child = {**__import__("os").environ, **opts.env}
115	    assert child["ANTHROPIC_API_KEY"] == "" and child["ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL"] == ""
116	    assert child["CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN"] == "test-subscription-token"
117	
118	
119	def test_t3_wires_subprocess_sandbox_settings(fake_sdk):
120	    # #125: the subprocess sandbox flags must actually reach the options (no tools, no settings
121	    # sources, non-interactive permission mode).
122	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("pong")]))
123	    _run()
124	    opts = fake_sdk.captured_options
125	    assert opts.allowed_tools == []  # no tools may run in a reasoning call
126	    assert opts.setting_sources == []  # do not load ambient project/user settings
127	    assert opts.permission_mode == "dontAsk"  # non-interactive, deny-by-default
128	
129	
130	def test_t3_hung_subprocess_is_cancelled_not_leaked(fake_sdk, monkeypatch):
131	    # #127: a hung stream must be cancelled by the inner timeout (frees the pool slot) and
132	    # surface a typed ReasoningError — not block forever.
133	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "HIGH_STAKES_TIMEOUT", 0.3)
134	    fake_sdk.append(_HANG)
135	    import time as _t
136	
137	    t0 = _t.monotonic()
138	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
139	        _run()
140	    assert _t.monotonic() - t0 < 5  # cancelled promptly near the 0.3s inner timeout, not hung
141	
142	
143	def test_t3_empty_reply_raises_transient(fake_sdk):
144	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([]))  # no text blocks -> empty
145	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
146	        _run()
147	
148	
149	def test_t3_rate_limit_event_rejected_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
150	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
151	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
152	        _run()
153	
154	
155	def test_t3_rate_limit_allowed_is_not_an_error(fake_sdk):
156	    # an informational 'allowed' rate event must NOT trigger backpressure; text still returns
157	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("allowed"))
158	    fake_sdk.append(_AssistantMessage([_TextBlock("ok")]))
159	    assert _run() == "ok"
160	
161	
162	def test_t3_auth_error_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
163	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=401, errors=["unauth"]))
164	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
165	        _run()
166	
167	
168	def test_t3_generic_error_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
169	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500, errors=["boom"]))
170	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
171	        _run()
172	
173	
174	def test_t3_429_not_clobbered_by_trailing_result_error(fake_sdk):
175	    # a RateLimitEvent(rejected) must win over a later ResultMessage error (preserve backpressure)
176	    fake_sdk.append(RateLimitEvent("rejected"))
177	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=500))
178	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
179	        _run()
180	
181	
182	def test_t3_overloaded_529_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
183	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=529, errors=["overloaded"]))
184	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
185	        _run()
186	
187	
188	def test_t3_forbidden_403_maps_to_permanent(fake_sdk):
189	    fake_sdk.append(_ResultMessage(is_error=True, api_error_status=403, errors=["forbidden"]))
190	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError):
191	        _run()
192	
193	
194	def test_t3_top_level_rejected_status_maps_to_ratelimit(fake_sdk):
195	    # a non-RateLimitEvent message carrying status=='rejected' is the second 429 path
196	    msg = types.SimpleNamespace(status="rejected")
197	    fake_sdk.append(msg)
198	    with pytest.raises(RateLimitError):
199	        _run()
200	
201	
202	def test_t3_sdk_raises_mid_stream_maps_to_transient(fake_sdk):
203	    # the SDK/transport raising (rather than completing the stream) is a transient failure
204	    fake_sdk.append(RuntimeError("connection reset"))
205	    with pytest.raises(ReasoningError):
206	        _run()
207	
208	
209	def test_t3_missing_auth_fails_loud(monkeypatch, fake_sdk):
210	    monkeypatch.delenv("CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN", raising=False)
211	    monkeypatch.setattr(rc, "_high_stakes_auth_present", lambda: False)
212	    with pytest.raises(PermanentReasoningError, match="subscription auth"):
213	        _run()

1	"""Study runner — the composed end-to-end pipeline (ADR-0010).
2	
3	Drives the engine's run_study over the synthetic reference adapter (tests/reference_study.py),
4	asserting the workflow composes correctly AND that its methodological/governance invariants hold
5	by construction: phase order, blind baseline, pre-registration before confirm, the governance
6	gate, and an append-only, tamper-evident provenance trail.

71	def res_verify_ok(res: StudyResult) -> None:
72	    verify_records(res.provenance)  # the trail is an intact hash chain
73	    kinds = [e.kind for e in res.provenance]
74	    assert kinds[0] == "run_start" and kinds[-1] == "report"  # closes with the report record
75	    # the REPORT phase was entered before the report record was written
76	    assert any(e.kind == "phase" and e.payload["phase"] == "REPORT" for e in res.provenance)
77	    assert res.baseline.corpus_fingerprint == res.corpus_fingerprint

83	def test_provenance_roundtrips_and_detects_tampering(tmp_path):
84	    res = _run(tmp_path, DISCOVERY)
85	    records = tuple(
86	        {
87	            "seq": e.seq,
88	            "kind": e.kind,
89	            "summary": e.summary,
90	            "payload": dict(e.payload),
91	            "timestamp": e.timestamp,
92	            "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
93	            "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
94	        }
95	        for e in res.provenance
96	    )
97	    assert from_records(records)  # intact chain rebuilds fine
98	    # edit a payload deep in the chain -> hash mismatch detected
99	    bad = list(records)
100	    bad[2] = {**bad[2], "summary": "TAMPERED"}

100	    bad[2] = {**bad[2], "summary": "TAMPERED"}
101	    with pytest.raises(ProvenanceError):
102	        from_records(bad)
103	    # drop an entry -> reorder/linkage detected
104	    with pytest.raises(ProvenanceError):
105	        from_records(records[:3] + records[4:])

142	def test_gate_review_payload_is_immutable_but_json_serializable(tmp_path):
143	    # #141: GateReview.payload is a deep-frozen FrozenDict (immutable, #113) — but the public
144	    # operator-review extension point must still be serializable. payload_dict() thaws it so an
145	    # operator can json.dumps the review without a custom encoder.
146	    import json
147	
148	    from assay_engine.pipeline import GateReview
149	
150	    review = GateReview(
151	        gate="g",
152	        frm=Phase.PREREGISTER,
153	        to=Phase.CONFIRM,
154	        summary="s",
155	        payload={"hypothesis_ids": ["h1", "h2"], "nested": {"k": 1}},
156	    )
157	    # raw payload is frozen (immutable) and NOT directly json-serializable
158	    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
159	        json.dumps(review.payload)
160	    # the thawed view round-trips through json cleanly
161	    out = json.loads(json.dumps(review.payload_dict()))
162	    assert out == {"hypothesis_ids": ["h1", "h2"], "nested": {"k": 1}}

197	def test_adjudication_scores_the_gated_claim_snapshot_not_a_remutated_source(tmp_path):
198	    # #95: a source returning different claims on successive .claims() calls must NOT let the gate
199	    # review one set while a different set is scored. The runner materializes once and scores that.
200	    from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord
201	
202	    calls = {"n": 0}
203	
204	    class Mutating:
205	        def claims(self):
206	            calls["n"] += 1
207	            cid = "c-A" if calls["n"] == 1 else "c-B"
208	            return [
209	                ClaimRecord(
210	                    claim_id=cid, subject=cid, referents=(cid,), assertion={"expected": "high"}
211	                )
212	            ]
213	
214	        def claim_fingerprint(self):
215	            return "fp"
216	
217	    src = ref.write_source(tmp_path / "c.json")
218	    plan = ref.make_plan(src, modes=ADJUDICATE)
219	    plan = replace(plan, definition=replace(plan.definition, claims_source=Mutating()))
220	    seen = {}
221	
222	    def handler(review):
223	        seen["ids"] = list(review.payload["claim_ids"])
224	        return GateDecision(approved=True, gate=review.gate, reason="ok")
225	
226	    res = run_study(plan, gate_handler=handler)
227	    scored = {v.hypothesis_id for v in res.scorecard.verdicts}
228	    assert seen["ids"] == ["c-A"]  # the gate reviewed the materialized snapshot
229	    assert scored == {"H-c-A"}  # and exactly that snapshot was scored (not c-B)

254	def test_recorded_claim_fingerprint_is_engine_computed_over_scored_claims(tmp_path):
255	    # #137: the recorded claim_fingerprint must be the engine's own hash of the EXACT claims
256	    # scored — not the source's unverified self-report. A source lying about its fingerprint must
257	    # not corrupt the trail's claim identity.
258	    from assay_engine.contracts.claims import ClaimRecord
259	    from assay_engine.pipeline import _engine_claim_fingerprint
260	
261	    records = [
262	        ClaimRecord(claim_id="c1", subject="c1", referents=("c1",), assertion={"e": "high"}),
263	        ClaimRecord(claim_id="c2", subject="c2", referents=("c2",), assertion={"e": "low"}),
264	    ]
265	    lying = type(
266	        "Lying",
267	        (),
268	        {"claims": lambda self: list(records), "claim_fingerprint": lambda self: "0" * 64},
269	    )()
270	    plan = ref.make_plan(ref.write_source(tmp_path / "c.json"), modes=ADJUDICATE)
271	    plan = replace(plan, definition=replace(plan.definition, claims_source=lying))
272	    seen = {}
273	
274	    def handler(review):
275	        seen.update(review.payload_dict())
276	        return GateDecision(approved=True, gate=review.gate, reason="ok")
277	
278	    run_study(plan, gate_handler=handler)
279	    assert seen["claim_fingerprint"] == _engine_claim_fingerprint(records)  # engine-computed
280	    assert seen["source_reported_claim_fingerprint"] == "0" * 64  # self-report kept for cross-check
281	    assert seen["claim_fingerprint_matches_source"] is False  # mismatch surfaced, not hidden

167	# ---- confirm_whole_corpus: direction-aware (#2) + engine-measured stability (#21) ----
168	
169	_STABLE_HI = [100.0] * 20  # resamples far above any small null -> stable for "greater"
170	_STABLE_LO = [-100.0] * 20  # far below -> stable for "less"
171	
172	
173	def test_whole_corpus_rejects_invalid_predicted_direction():
174	    # #128: an unrecognized direction must RAISE, not silently fall through to the 'less' tail
175	    # (which would flip supported<->contradicted). Both the locked-direction and the
176	    # confirm-time-argument paths must validate.
177	    bad = _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS, predicted_direction="up")
178	    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="predicted_direction must be one of"):
179	        confirm_whole_corpus(bad, observed=10.0, null_distribution=[0.0] * 100, alpha=0.05)
180	    ok = _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS)  # no locked direction
181	    with pytest.raises(ValueError, match="predicted_direction must be one of"):
182	        confirm_whole_corpus(
183	            ok,
184	            observed=10.0,
185	            null_distribution=[0.0] * 100,
186	            alpha=0.05,
187	            predicted_direction="sideways",
188	        )
189	
190	
191	def test_whole_corpus_stability_requires_reproducing_the_observed_effect():
192	    # #139: resamples that clear the null median by an epsilon (reproducing only the effect SIGN,
193	    # not its magnitude) must NOT earn stability=1.0 / SUPPORTED. Stability must measure that each
194	    # resample, scored against the null, is itself significant in the predicted tail.
195	    null = list(range(0, 21))  # median 10
196	    barely = [10.0001] * 100  # just above the null median, far below the observed effect of 100
197	    v = confirm_whole_corpus(
198	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
199	        observed=100.0,
200	        null_distribution=null,
201	        alpha=0.05,
202	        resample_statistics=barely,
203	        predicted_direction="greater",
204	    )
205	    assert v.label is VerdictLabel.INDETERMINATE  # pre-fix: SUPPORTED with stability=1.0
206	    assert v.evidence["stability"] == 0.0  # no resample reproduces the observed effect
207	    # a genuinely reproducing resample distribution (each significant vs the null) is still stable
208	    strong = [100.0] * 100
209	    v2 = confirm_whole_corpus(
210	        _locked(HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS),
211	        observed=100.0,
212	        null_distribution=null,
213	        alpha=0.05,
214	        resample_statistics=strong,
215	        predicted_direction="greater",
216	    )
217	    assert v2.label is VerdictLabel.SUPPORTED and v2.evidence["stability"] == 1.0

396	        def start_run(self, name, params):

398	            return "run-1"

578	    # must be rejected (not_after=baseline_instant). The reference locks in the past (accepted);
579	    # here we lock on-demand at now() inside hypothesis_for → must raise.
580	    from assay_engine.methodology.preregistration import lock_hypothesis

583	    plan = ref.make_plan(src, modes=ADJUDICATE)

587	            hypothesis_id=f"H-{claim.claim_id}",
588	            statement="s",
589	            kind=HypothesisKind.WHOLE_CORPUS,
590	            origin=HypothesisOrigin.EXTERNAL_CLAIM,
591	            test_name="t",
592	            decision_rule="r",
593	            source_claim_id=claim.claim_id,
594	            predicted_direction="greater",
